itemListElement.reviewBody:
Overall this school is okay, but there are a lot of problems here. Im a former-student in this school. One of the problems are the benches are very small for classes 6th to 8th (9th and 10th class have more comfortable benches). Next is late notice, always the official school group in whatsapp notifies us very late on what and when to do (for example like some events or contests). Next problem is over-population, over 2000 students study here and bus transport is also over-populated (because of which some students have to stand the whole bus ride). Extreme noise pollution is cause because of faulty equipment in this school like doors slamming. Another problem is language, the school says its a english-medium school but everyone here speaks in hindi (even some teachers dont know basic english). The exams are not taken seriously by techers and staff, some teachers are absent when its exam day and if students have doubt it cant be cleared. Parent Teacher Meeting are counducted 2 times an acedemic year but its not seriously taken by teacher its just like a formality here. Only 1 ground which is full of dusty sand and rocks (and so when it rains it becomes mud), is too less for a school with this many students. The ground has very low walls that keep the schools territory away from the road, but the problem is that the football or volleyball flies way above the short wall, and so students have to ask strangers from outside to throw the ball back in or go and get it themselves (which is not safe, and students are given permission from careless 2 or 3 security in the gate). Most students here hate this school, and want to leave but cant due to financial problems or parental disagreement. The school has some subjects such as vedic maths, computer science, cca, etc but these subjects happen very rarely due to very low faculty of teachers (and them being absent). High fees are paid but you still have to pay for more extra things (whatever it may be that is required, according to the school). It has also come to my attention that teachers are forced to complete portions of both techno and cbse (which is way to much for their age) and they dont care if the students understand or not. Notebook corrections are not taken seriously. The extremely loud sound from the school events affect over 5 apartments around this school ( i dont know how government approved this exact location for a school). Shortage of school uniform, book and other necessities for students at yhe start of the year is also a huge problem. School assemblies are done under the school (also known as ground floor) which becomes very humid and sweaty. The principal, vice principal and dean are just enjoying the money they get from scamming the parents of these kinds into thinking that this will help their students in the future. They have zero responsibility and dont care about the parents, school, students or even faculty. I know this because my parents had complained to the school several times but did not get a response nor did the problem get resolved what so ever.
Some rooms are not even used in the academic year like the computer lab, science lab, library, etc. A lot of faulty things in the school such as benches, walls, restrooms, etc. It is very easy for students to do prohibited stuff such as sneaking out of class, running, bullying, fighting, etc. Extremely poor infrastructure for a sri chaitanya school. No competitions conducted in schools at all. So my conclusion is dont get admissioned into this school...

Irresponsible teachers, not quality teachings, Illustrate and uncultured non teaching staffs.
Careless admin no regulations.

Please 🙏 stop this loud music , it's from morning now it's 9:35 please allow us some peace!!

As a resident in the vicinity of Sri Chaitanya School I feel compelled to share my experience regarding the excessive noise generated by the school's recent annual function. While I understand and appreciate the importance of such events for the school community, the extreme volume of the music and festivities has significantly disrupted the peace and tranquility of our residential area.
The noise emanating from the school's premises every day and now during the event is unbearable, reaching levels that far exceeded what one would expect in a residential neighbourhood. It is causing disturbance not only to myself but to many other residents in the area who value a peaceful environment for their homes.
Also, exposing children to prolonged periods of high-volume noise can have adverse effects on their hearing health, potentially leading to long-term consequences. Moreover, the disruptive nature of the noise can disrupt their sleep patterns and overall sense of comfort, affecting their ability to concentrate in school and perform academically.
I see other people have made the same complaint but you have not taken any action against this. This is very shameful as you call yourself a school but totally disregard other's feelings to have a peaceful environment. What are you teaching your kids there? I am sure it's not morals.

The wagholi school is in the residential complex and every single morning they play music so loudly, it is now a nuisance. What civic sense is the school teaching to future citizens and the person answering the phone when I called up to complain was so arrogant about it that he was hell bent on knowing exactly which flat I am residing and my name rather than acknowledging the nuisance they are causing. It is a everyday occurrence now. If this is how administrators are in this school, i wonder what kind of citizens they are creating

Your claim of being a "school" is ironic when you neglect teaching children to respect neighbors' boundaries, evident in the loud music played daily. Is it not too loud even for you? My windows closed, yet I hear "kyun paisa paisa karti hai!?" Is this the music promoting respect? As a school, tasked with imparting knowledge, the loud noise contradicts the importance you should place on education. How can students focus in such an environment? You're sending the wrong message.
Its techno school and its curriculum designed for IIT and medical examinations
